name: Cypress Hills Local Development Corporation Chldc
description_short: Cypress Hills LDC is a Brooklyn-based nonprofit providing free career, education, housing counseling, youth programs, and community organizing services to East New York residents.
description_long:
Cypress Hills Local Development Corporation (CHLDC) is a community-based nonprofit organization serving the East New York neighborhood of Brooklyn. Founded with a mission to build equitable community development, the organization is led by incoming Executive Director Michael G. Johnson (effective February 2, 2026) and operates multiple divisions addressing educational access, economic mobility, affordable housing, and youth development.

CHLDC offers comprehensive free services across six main program areas: Career & Education services for skill development and employment goals; College Success programs providing college access and persistence support for high school and college students; Community Organizing support for resident advocates of all ages; Housing Counseling for homeowners and tenants seeking affordable housing access and maintenance; Youth & Family Services including after-school programs, summer camps, and enrichment activities; and Community Development initiatives focused on increasing affordable housing and energy efficiency. All after-school programs are provided at no cost to participating school communities.

The organization distinguishes itself through deep community integration, operating 14 school-based after-school programs in partnership with Community School District 19, running Beacon Community Centers at multiple school sites, and maintaining the Middle School Student Success Center to guide middle school students through high school applications. CHLDC explicitly centers racial equity in its mission and actively promotes policies advancing equitable outcomes. Programming is specifically tailored to East New York residents with documented attention to family engagement through events and resource fairs.

As a legitimate nonprofit with established programs and named leadership, CHLDC provides genuine free services without financial products or predatory lending. The organization's scope is geographically limited to East New York/Cypress Hills Brooklyn, and specific program details (contact persons, addresses, registration processes) are provided transparently on their website. This is an appropriate free-help resource for Brooklyn residents seeking nonfinancial community support services.
pros: All after-school programs are completely free to participating school communities
14 school-based partnerships in Community School District 19 provide widespread access
Summer Rising programs operate 7-8 weeks at no cost with extended hours (8am-6pm for elementary)
Housing Counseling specifically addresses affordable housing access and tenant/homeowner support
College Success programs provide persistence support through college graduation, not just admission
Explicitly committed to racial equity and policy advocacy beyond direct service provision
Clear staff contacts and specific program locations provided for easy access and accountability
cons: Services are geographically limited to East New York/Cypress Hills Brooklyn area only
Youth programs appear primarily focused on K-8th grade, with limited middle/high school specifics
Website lacks detailed eligibility criteria or income thresholds for housing counseling services
No information about wait lists, capacity limitations, or application procedures for most programs
Limited details on specific outcomes, success metrics, or program evaluation data available
best_for: Brooklyn East New York families seeking free after-school and summer enrichment programs for children
High school and college students from the area needing college access and persistence support
Homeowners and tenants requiring affordable housing counseling and displacement prevention assistance
Community residents interested in organizing and advocacy training around neighborhood issues
diagnosis: CHLDC is appropriate for East New York Brooklyn residents seeking free community-based support in education, youth enrichment, housing counseling, or community organizing—not for credit, lending, or financial product needs. The main caveat is that services are strictly geographic (East New York only) and do not address consumer finance issues; this is a legitimate nonprofit service provider, not a credit or lending resource.
services: Free after-school programs (K-8, 3 hours daily, 5 days per week) at 14 school sites
Summer Rising programs for elementary and middle school students (7-8 weeks, 8am-6pm)
Homework help, literacy, STEM, arts, sports, and wellness activities in after-school settings
Middle School Student Success Center (MSSSC) for high school application navigation
College access and persistence services for high school and college students
Housing counseling for homeowners and tenants seeking affordable housing
Community organizing training and advocacy support for residents
Youth and family enrichment programs, events, and resource fairs
Beacon Community Center recreational and educational programming for all ages
Career and education services for adults seeking employment and skill development
Full-day programming on 13 school holidays throughout the year
Family events and performances through school-based programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
